title: Reasoning, Planning, Decomposition, and Verification · Independent Prompt Trial
summary: Prompt a difficult migration without exposing or demanding hidden reasoning. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a plan-execute-verify task contract with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Request concise conclusions, evidence, and checks rather than hidden chain-of-thought disclosure.
Use planning before implementation when scope, dependencies, or user choices are materially uncertain.
Decompose work around independently verifiable outcomes and dependency order.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
Use a changed lab case derived from this module: prompt reasoning planning. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed lab case derived from this module: prompt reasoning planning. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. What must be established before implementation?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for request concise conclusions, evidence, and checks rather than hidden chain-of-thought disclosure.

content.text: Request concise conclusions, evidence, and checks rather than hidden chain-of-thought disclosure. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: prompt reasoning planning. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Reasoning Selection, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a plan-execute-verify task contract.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Reliable reasoning requires the model to print every private thought. This fails the Reasoning Selection contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: prompt-uncertainty-report-1y0wfz" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Uncertainty Report into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
Evidence: prompt-uncertainty-report-1y0wfz
Goal: [replace with one observable outcome]
Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

stimulus.title: Uncertainty Report failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: A confident answer should never mention uncertainty.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Require bounded uncertainty, missing evidence, and blockers to remain visible.

options.text: Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Uncertainty Report condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder.
Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Uncertainty Report applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Require bounded uncertainty, missing evidence, and blockers to remain visible.
